statement_to_voters:
I'm honoured to be a councillor candidate for Barking Abbey ward from the Conservative Party. Having lived in Barking for sixteen years, I understand the unique challenges our streets face every day. We deserve better. My goal is simple: a stronger, safer, and cleaner neighbourhood where every resident feels heard. On 7th May 2026, you have a choice. Vote for a candidate who will fight for a council tax freeze, tackle anti-social behaviour, lower your parking charges and always put your voice first.
Your Borough. Your vote. Your Voice.

statement_to_voters:
Alibon deserves better.

I've lived in Dagenham since 2004, and I'm standing to be your councillor because residents are being let down.

Higher council tax. Dirtier streets. Rising parking fees. No accountability.

I'm not a politician - I'm a safety‑critical professional who wants to bring responsibility and real representation back to Alibon ward.

It's time for a councillor who listens and acts.

It's time for change in Alibon.

We need:

* Safer, Clean Streets and Parks.
* A freeze in Council Tax
* Reversal of the 51% increase in the cost of Residents Parking Permits.
* Only Essential CPZs (Controlled Parking Zones)

statement_to_voters:
I am Shahana H S Choudhury, LLB (Hons), LPC and I am honoured to stand as the Conservative Party candidate and currently serve as the Deputy Chairman for Membership and Finance within the party.

With a professional background in law and actively working online with UW. I also act as an Involvement Representative (IR) with NELFT that is affiliated with the NHS, and I work as a Freelance Interpreter. I am a proud a resident of this area (Barking & Dagenham) for many years and I have contributed to local education as a school governor at Dorothy Barley Junior School and Jo Richardson Community School.

Being a mother pursuing a PhD and a proud mother to a daughter and a mother-in-law to a son-in-law who are also undertaking doctoral studies, I greatly value the significance of education. Having earned two academic degrees despite numerous challenges, I am committed to achieving my long-held aspiration of completing a PhD. This is a goal that I have cherished since my university days. I understand that education is very important and valuable in all aspects of life and it’s a capital for an individual too.

As a committed member of Barking and Dagenham, I am dedicated to making a positive impact in our community if elected councillor.

Commitment to Public Service

Serving as a Councillor has been a longstanding ambition of mine. This aspiration reflects my dedication to making a positive impact within the community and my firm belief in the importance of public service. I am eager to contribute meaningfully, drawing on my experiences and commitment to help shape the future of our area.

My qualifications include extensive legal expertise, medical insight gained through my NHS involvement, significant experience in interpreting, facilitating clear communication across language barriers.

Growing up in a Conservative constituency (Southend) I am confident that my dedication, professional abilities and commitment to public service will be of great benefit to the area. I am wholly committed to campaigning for constructive changes.

statement_to_voters: I’m Kash Haroon and I’m standing to be Labour’s candidate for the constituency of West Worcestershire. West Worcestershire is a great area with the potential to be even greater. What we need here is a Labour MP working with a Labour government – we need investment and a vision to make West Worcestershire a vibrant county.   I’ve been a member of the Labour Party for nearly 15 years and am a proud member of the Unite the Union. I have served as a local Councillor for 10 years and am the Cabinet Member for Public Realm & Climate Change at the London Borough of Barking and Dagenham. By profession I am a qualified solicitor.

statement_to_voters:
I have had the privilege of living in this borough for nearly twenty years, I am a hardworking dedicated person, deeply committed to my community and pursuing its best interests. I have worked for Transport for London for nearly twenty-six years and I have served this community for nearly twenty years through my job as well as through various community work with children and young people with special needs, mental health issues and drug related issues. I myself am a father of a special needs son and I have lot of experience dealing with special needs children. I believe this borough needs to improve immensely in dealing with their needs.

I am honoured to be running for the position of councillor of the Mayesbrook Ward for the Conservative Party. I am passionate about improving on local areas. I will be committed to acting as a strong collaborative voice for the resilient residents of Mayesbrook Ward as well as the whole borough of Barking and Dagenham. Council tax is another big issue for our borough, if Local Conservatives are elected we will freeze council tax.

We will make our ward as well as the borough a safer and a cleaner place as well as improving parking availability for our local residents. I am also deeply concerned about drug use and abuse by the young people of our borough and many of them are suffering. And if we are elected we will take this issue very seriously and we will support these young people in the best way possible. If elected we hope to work closely with law enforcement to improve crime prevention in our borough.

I am committed to listening to residents, representing your concerns and working hard to deliver solutions that make a real difference.

statement_to_voters:
I’ve lived in Barnet all my life and in Underhill for over 24 years, so I know the area - and the issues - extremely well. Over that time, I’ve worked closely with local residents, representing their concerns and helping to drive real change in our community.

In 2010, working alongside residents, I led a successful five-year campaign to protect local public open space from development, securing its registration as a Village Green following a public inquiry. Since then, I’ve continued to protect our green spaces and am now working to bring the long-derelict former Quinta Club building back into community use.

More recently, I’ve worked with residents to challenge unfair parking schemes, including Controlled Parking Zones introduced without clear evidence, and excessive parking charges at local shops that are harming small businesses. Through sustained pressure, we have already reduced the scale of one scheme and continue to push for fairer, evidence-based solutions.

I’ve also supported residents affected by stalled regeneration at Dollis Valley, worked with Barnet Hospital and the Council on parking pressures, and consistently challenged inappropriate development - particularly on Green Belt land or where it fails to reflect the character of the area. I support more genuinely affordable housing, but it must be in the right places and designed properly.

I have also represented residents for years on the local Community Action Panel, working with police and councillors to help set neighbourhood policing priorities, including tackling burglary, vehicle crime and antisocial behaviour.

I currently serve as Chair of the Quinta Village Green Residents’ Association and previously served as a school governor at Whitings Hill Primary School, including five years as Chair. I am also a local parent and run a family business in the renewable energy sector.

I’m standing as an Independent because I believe local decisions should be driven by what’s right for Underhill - not by party politics. For too long, our area has been represented by councillors who don’t live here and don’t fully understand local needs.

Many residents already know me through the work we’ve done together. I’m not just a name on a ballot paper - I’ve been actively working for this community for years. I’m now asking for your support to continue that work from within the Council, so I can do even more for Underhill.

statement_to_voters:
My name is Ashlynn and I live and work in Brookvale and Kings Furlong. I grew up near Alton and have been a visitor to Basingstoke since childhood and have lived and worked here for nearly 3 years. I work for the NHS in the drug and alcohol service supporting the people of Basingstoke and further afield in accessing recovery.
I grew up through financial crashes and failing local services that let more and more of us fall through the cracks each year.

I feel that the old two party system has been letting us down as long as I've been alive and am keen to represent the Green party in our bold vision for a politics of hope that can steer our area and nation back on track. This way we can look after eachother in order to live comfortable lives.

Temilola has been a Bexley resident for eight years who has built strong community roots both personally and professionally. She is a primary school teacher passionate about early development, helping children build confidence, resilience and a love of learning. She also runs roller skating festivals and mentoring workshops for young people.

party_description_text: Working for Sidcup - Not Party HQ

statement_to_voters:
### **To the People of St Mary’s & St James **

I’ve called Bexley home for over 15 years. I chose to raise my three daughters here because of our strong community, green spaces, and the sense of safety we used to have. But like you, I’ve watched standards slip year after year.

**Bexley Council has had it too easy for too long. Reform UK will change that.**  
I’m standing because our borough deserves better services, sensible development, safer streets, and a council that respects your money.

#### **I see the decline every day — and I know you do too**

As a professional driver, I’m out on our roads daily. I see the potholes that damage cars and endanger cyclists. I see the fly-tipping blighting places like North cray Road , Vicarage Road sitting there for weeks. I see how abandoned areas attract antisocial behaviour.

And as a parent, I worry. I want my daughters — and all our children — to grow up in a Bexley where they feel safe walking home, where parks aren’t vandalised, and where the council actually deals with problems.

**Council tax up. Standards down. Enough.**

Your bills have gone up, but has Bexley got better?  
- Rubbish left uncollected
- Roads crumbling  
- Local facilities neglected

**Reform UK are the taxpayers’ watchdog.** Where we’ve won seats across the country, we’ve focused on one thing: fixing the basics and giving residents value for money. I’ll bring that same focus here.

#### **Protect Bexley, don’t concrete over it**

I love this borough — its history, its green spaces, its community feel. That’s why I’ll fight:
- **No** to over-development without the roads, schools, and GP surgeries to support it
- **Yes** to protecting our parks and open spaces
- **Yes** to planning that puts Bexley residents before developers  
- **Yes** to tackling fly-tipping and antisocial behaviour with proper enforcement and CCTV in hotspots
- **Yes** to backing Bexley village— sorting parking, improving transport, and helping local shops thrive

#### **It’s time to send a message**

Labour and the Conservatives have had their chance. The result? Broken promises, debt, rising crime, uncontrolled migration, and a country that feels less secure. They’ve stopped listening to people like you.

**Reform UK offers a real alternative.** We have the vision and the backbone to put Great back into Britain — starting right here in Bexley.

On May 7th, you can start that change.

**A vote for Reform UK is a vote for a better Bexley and a better Britain.**

**I WILL listen. I WILL take action. Your voice WILL be heard.**

---

statement_to_voters:
Roxanne has worked in project management for a range of charities and non-profits, delivering projects that benefit communities. She has led environmental initiatives and volunteered with local organisations for years. Roxanne is committed to doing things with communities, not to them, ensuring residents’ voices are heard.

Roxanne is committed to protecting and investing in our parks and green spaces, so more young people and families can enjoy them. Drawing on her experience in project management for charities, Roxanne would work alongside local voluntary groups to help realise community-led plans to improve sports facilities at Rowheath Pavilion and develop a wellbeing centre at Claypits Pool.

Growing up in Erdington, I can see first-hand the impact of Tory austerity and Labour council cuts on our community. In standing on a platform of anti-cuts and anti-war, I will work with local residents, trade unions and public sector staff to fight for the services and funding we need.

Money can be found for bombs and dodgy PPE deals, but not for our streets or our youth. This is why we need someone in Westminster who will raise the voice of ordinary workers while also linking it to the need for a new mass worker’s party in the interests of the many.

Imran has lived in Hodge Hill for most of his life and attended Hodge Hill School. He has worked in education for over 12 years, represented parents as school governor, volunteered with the Youth Offending Service and also acted as a local football coach.

Having been a member of the Green Party for over six years, Imran stood as a local candidate previously. He is standing as a Parliamentary candidate now because he is passionate about social and environmental justice. He will campaign to highlight local issues which have been neglected, such as the poor standards of the local environment and the safety issues. Imran says:

“I will work to eliminate fly-tipping and keep our streets and parks clean, and make Birmingham’s roads safer by putting an end to speeding and knife-crime. Our libraries must stay open, and investment in our public services must continue. The cost-of-living crisis is affecting more and more of us. I want a fairer deal for us all.

“The ongoing crisis in the Middle East has highlighted the failure of our government to uphold international law and human rights. This must be addressed as it goes against our moral and ethical standards of upholding civil liberties and human rights for all.”
I am standing as an Independent candidate for Handsworth because I believe our community deserves a councillor who listens, acts, and puts the community first.

We’re proud to call Birmingham home. Ladywood is one of the most vibrant and diverse communities in our city, but we also know that too many residents feel unheard, especially when it comes to regeneration, housing, and the everyday issues that affect our streets.

After the Conservatives took £1bn from our city, we know things have been tough. But with a Labour government backing councils, we’ve secured over £650m in new funding to protect and rebuild our vital services.

We’ll always put Brummies and Ladywood first - helping residents with the cost of living and backing our young people.

Labour are introducing breakfast clubs, expanding free school meals and taking 60,000 children in Birmingham out of poverty. And your Labour council is putting £1m into youth services.

We’ve been out over the past year – not just at election time – listening to residents, standing up for our community, and pushing for fair solutions shaped with local people.

Based on these conversations, we’ve developed some key priorities:

We’ll push for fair, transparent regeneration that protects Ladywood’s identity 
 
We'll tackle poorly managed HMOs and Secure Exempt Accommodation with action against rogue landlords

We'll Improve the bin service and clean up our streets – tackling fly‑tipping with increased enforcement

And we’ll invest in our community – reopening the neighbourhood office, supporting local businesses, improving safety for women and girls, and making sure growth works for local people.

Labour will bring unity to our city and challenge division wherever it appears. There are others in this election trying to divide us, pit neighbours against each other and talk Birmingham down.

We know Birmingham is strongest when we stand together.

We care deeply about Ladywood, and we’re determined to work with residents, listen, and represent you.

We’d be honoured to have your support.

Sally and Satvir.

“I was born and raised in the West Midlands, and I am proud to have made Ladywood my home. I love being part of such a vibrant, diverse community, and I want to help make it better.

“I have worked in homeless services in our area and seen firsthand the harm done by Tory cuts and Labour’s poor governance. I know we need to work hard to improve our public services. I am ready to do that work.

"I have signed the Safer streets for Birmingham campaign pledge, and both the Brummies united against racism and Palestinian solidarity candidate pledges. Equality is at the heart of my politics and I will always stand up for marginalised people here in Ladywood and abroad.

"In Ladywood I will campaign for cleaner safer streets, improved public services, better regulation to prevent rogue landlords from abusing tenants, and will campaign from day one to get around the negotiating table to end the Birmingham bin strikes.

"I feel the Ladywood Regeneration has been handled abysmally, and I will campaign from day one for the consultation to restart with residents at the heart of planning and transparency in all aspects of the project.

“We need a bolder politics. Labour have spent years ignoring Ladywood and failing our whole city. It’s time for change. Vote Green!”

13 years of Labour mismanagement has made Birmingham unrecognisable. It is time for the people to take back control with robust and honest representation, securing a return to cleaner and safer streets, a return for enterprise and business and secure hosing and social services.

Having grown up in Hay Mills and lived her for two decades, I am deeply invested in making our streets cleaner and safer for the community. I will fight to end the bin strikes, protect our vital public services, contribute to city-wide policies that affect the ward, and work to bring the fresh investment the city deserves.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

PEOPLE FIRST
SAFER STREETS
YOUTH CENTRES
CLAMP DOWN ON DRUGD

Hello, I am Councillor Safina Kauser, your local councillor for Manningham Ward, proud to represent our community and standing for re-election in May 2026 to continue the work we have already started together.

I was born and raised in Manningham, educated locally, and now live and raise my family here. I am also a qualified lecturer with over 15 years’ experience in education.

Since being elected in May 2023, I have focused on the issues that matter most to residents, including SEND, fly-tipping, potholes, blocked gullies, street lighting and unadopted roads, taking action through days of action, working with council teams and key stakeholders, delivering real results.

Residents have raised concerns about safety, clean streets, opportunities for young people and pressure on local services. I have acted on these issues, working to protect the community, improve local environments, support youth initiatives and make sure residents’ voices are heard in council decisions.

Alongside this, I have protected vital services such as Manningham Sports Centre and Midland Road tip, remained accessible through surgeries, ward walks, phone and email, and continue to support residents behind the scenes with housing, school appeals and access to services, remaining committed to delivering on the pledges I made to Manningham.

I am asking for your support in the May 2026 election so I can continue serving our community and delivering results for Manningham.

After speaking with many residents and local businesses across Manningham, I’ve been encouraged to stand in the local elections on 7 May 2026.

I’m proud to be selected as a Labour candidate for Manningham Ward.

Having lived in Manningham most of my life, I understand our community and the challenges we face. I have spent decades working in youth and community development across Bradford, and I am a local businessman, charity director and General Secretary of a Masjid in Frizinghall.

I know residents are concerned about crime, fly-tipping, opportunities for young people and pressure on local services. If elected, I will work to make our streets safer, improve cleanliness, support young people and ensure Manningham’s voice is heard.

This election is about our community and our future. Together, we can build a safer, cleaner, stronger Manningham.

I would be honoured to have your support.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.
See links below for candidate's local election 2026 info.

I am a local resident and business owner. I want us and especially our children to feel safer on our streets as I have seen a surge in shoplifting and knife crime in the past few years. I also would like to see more good shops open on Kilburn High Road and a better outlook for businesses.

Having come to this country for university nearly 15 years ago, for the last few years, I have
proudly made Bromley my home. Having lived in various parts of the borough I currently live
right in the town centre which is also the ward I am running in. All of us who are Bromley
residents should be proud of living in one of the greenest boroughs in London and should
fight to keep it that way at all costs. Granted, those who work multiple jobs or cut back on
basic necessities to make ends meet this may not find this a priority. I recognize that green
initiatives locally and nationally must go hand in hand with fair and just economic system
which I believe that the Green Party offers. Bromley town centre is beautiful and vibrant, but
we all have seen the devastating effects of drug addiction in the ward especially on the high
street. The current drug policy has failed to address this issue adequately, so I believe that
the Green Party approach of treating drug addiction as a public health issue is the only way
forward. I have previous experience of working in community development outside the
borough and I have first hand experience of seeing the impact that local projects have on the
wellbeing of a community. I am staunchly anti-racist and I have previously volunteered for
anti-racism organizations. I aim to represent all residents of Bromley town, work hard to
maintain our green spaces, improve our social services and guarantee equal access to them
for all residents.

Having lived in Kelsey and Eden Park ward my entire life, I am deeply familiar with the concerns of local residents. Since joining the Green Party last year, I have been proud to represent the interests of young people and families under a banner I trust to stand up for ordinary people and reflect their voices in decision-making.

As your representative on Bromley Council, I would prioritise road safety, particularly on Wickham Road and Upper Elmers End Road, which have become hotspots for dangerous driving. I would advocate the introduction of 20mph speed limits on the most dangerous roads, ensuring that Bromley aligns with the 2022 changes to the Highway Code, which place greater responsibility on motorists and prioritise pedestrians at crossings and junctions. Fatal and life-changing accidents have no place on our streets, and I would work to reduce the risks on our roads.

As a volunteer at Dorset Road Allotments, I understand the value of protecting our green spaces, including parks and nature reserves. I would campaign for renewed investment in Kelsey Park, reversing years of Conservative neglect. The closure of the main bridge since 2021, with restoration works only commencing in January 2026, is emblematic of wider challenges facing Bromley: continued service reductions alongside repeated council tax increases.

I believe the time has come to seek change at council level, guided by the Green Party's commitment to an equitable society, where working people can thrive rather than feeling the pinch.

Ju has several years of casework experience giving her a strong understanding of the issues people may need support with. She studied International Politics and Military History at University. She has previously worked in retail and enjoys volunteering in the local community.

Ju will stand up for the residents Kelsey and Eden Park and be a strong representative on Bromley Council if elected, regularly engaging with residents.

As a resident, it often feels like St Paul's Cray has been neglected and that's why I'm standing up.
We need people serious about our community, passionate and able to deliver on their pledges—not those who make endless excuses. I have a PhD in Politics, lectured the subject at several universities, but more importantly, I am motivated to bring real change & expertise to the issues that matter to all of us as residents– for instance, tackling flytipping, antisocial behaviour, crime, protecting greenbelt, local services and improving local schools.

Gerda has lived in the ward since 1985, where she brought up her family and served as a school governor. Now retired, she has been a volunteer for HealthWatch Bromley, a trained visitor for Care and Nursing homes and chairs the Patient Participation Group at Chelsfield Surgery. In her spare time she enjoys tending her garden and sings with several local choirs.

Florine has lived in Broxbourne for a decade. She is passionate about making Cheshunt a safe and clean place for good health and quality of life. As a mum of young children who also has ageing parents, she knows that the cost of living and access to good local services matter.

I have been a youth worker for eight years and I am a mum. I love living in Bury and I want to give back to my community.

I am excited to be running as a candidate in Bury West. I am just a normal person who wants to make a positive difference. I offer a fresh perspective and hope in making politics less volatile.

I want to be a councillor who is visible and accessible. I want to work in partnership with local business, schools, community organisations and with you the residents. I will listen to your views, help and represent this constituency effectively at Bury Council.

My name is Zach Campbell-Robinson, I have lived in Ramsbottom for the majority of my life, and work in a local restaurant, which has given me insight into the issues facing locals.

I am standing as a Reform UK councillor to ensure these issues are fixed, such as fixing our roads and protecting our greenbelt.
I firmly believe in cutting waste, so your hard-earned taxpayer money goes to meaningful action, all of which will contribute to protecting Bury’s future and taking it in the right direction.

I am standing as a candidate because my work as a teacher in the local community has given me a close understanding of the challenges people face, especially those from disadvantaged backgrounds. I have seen first-hand how years of underfunding have impacted essential services, particularly for young people and families. I want to use my experience to help create a stronger, more inclusive, and sustainable community.

I support the Radcliffe and Redvales Flood Defence Scheme, which will protect up to 870 properties from a repeat of the devastating flooding seen on Boxing Day 2015. I also support increasing green spaces and amenity areas, including improved sports facilities for the community. Through my work, I have seen the challenges many people face—from barriers to learning to food poverty—made worse by rising food and petrol prices. I want to help create a fairer, more supportive, and sustainable community for everyone. I am committed to supporting families through inclusive education and accessible services, tackling food poverty and waste, and helping to rebuild strong, connected community networks.

party_name: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

statement_to_voters:
I’ve called Cherry Hinton home for 11 years, and I know the power of everyday community. In 2022, I organised a Front Door Festival that brought neighbours together for the first time in decades, sharing food, music, laughter and raising funds for the Cherry Hinton Hub.

I’m standing because I believe every street can feel like that: connected, creative and caring. I dream of a Cherry Hinton where there's a Front Door Festival in every street and neighbourhood. Where neighbours know each other and share skills, tools, meals, their time and joy.
Where we hear birdsong and the buzzing of bees instead of speeding motorbikes and the youth address their trauma through art and music instead of resorting to anti-social behaviour. Where the most vulnerable are looked after, making everyone else better off for it.

My strengths lie in organising, facilitating, empathising, envisioning and building community. My background spans human‑centred design in tech and hands‑on work with organisations like Jimmy’s Cambridge, improving services for people experiencing homelessness. I understand how systems, behaviour and trauma intersect, and why kindness and community matter more than ever as we face rising costs, extreme weather and growing instability.

We’re in the same boat, facing the same storms. But when we look after each other, we’re stronger.

Together, let us build a safe, strong, fair and vibrant Cherry Hinton.
Who's with me?

I am Dr. Ahmad Rushdhi, also known as Dr. Ru or DrAR, and I am the Liberal Democrat candidate for Cherry Hinton in Cambridge. Having moved to Cambridge from Sri Lanka in 2022, I have made Cherry Hinton my home. This has provided me with the opportunity to engage closely with local residents and gain a comprehensive understanding of the critical issues affecting our community.

As a physician specializing in clinical nutrition, my professional focus is on preventive medicine and public health. I am affiliated with Addenbrooke’s Hospital and also maintain a practice in London. My work is dedicated to disease prevention and promoting healthier lifestyles through education, nutrition, and early intervention. I firmly believe that enhancing public health extends beyond healthcare services to encompass the development of robust community well-being.

Since my arrival in Cherry Hinton, I have actively participated in community life, regularly interacting with residents from diverse backgrounds, including those employed in the NHS and other essential services.

Residents have communicated several practical concerns, including traffic congestion, road maintenance and potholes, and the enhancement of neighborhood safety. I am committed to collaborating with the council, police, and community partners to address these issues and implement effective solutions.

Additionally, concerns have been raised regarding anti-social behavior in the vicinity of certain Voi e-scooter parking areas, as well as knife crime, disruptive moped noise, and theft around local supermarkets. I will work in conjunction with neighborhood police teams and residents’ associations to support coordinated action and strengthen community safety initiatives.

Public health remains a paramount passion of mine. I collaborate closely with General Practitioners and other healthcare professionals, and I aspire to promote local community health awareness programs. Furthermore, I also share health education through my social media platforms.

If elected, my priorities will be to engage with residents, foster community cohesion, and contribute to the development of a healthier, safer, and more resilient Cherry Hinton.

Dr. Ahmad Rushdhi (Dr. Ru)
Liberal Democrat Candidate – Cherry Hinton
DrArLibdem@gmail.com

Ania has lived in Cambridge since 2015. Having grown up in Poland, Ania is one of many scientists who have come to Cambridge for work and decided to make it their permanent home.

Having worked for a Cambridge-based company for the past 10 years, and now taking on a freelance role, Ania is keen to make sure that Cambridge works for everyone equitably – residents, businesses, visitors and nature.

She is also passionate about evidence-based medicine, and using best available data to make healthcare decisions.

Her love of dance led her to volunteer for a local dance society to increase access to affordable community social classes.

In her spare time, Ania is learning to play the trumpet and trains with a local club in Latin Formation dancing. Ania is also a keen cyclist and loves going on walks with her corgi.

If elected she will work with Bob Illingworth and Ian Manning to stand up for East Chesterton residents and make your voice heard.

My name is Maruf Ahmed I am your Labour councillor candidate and
care deeply about our community. Through my work with the 
community, foodbank, and local charities, I’ve supported 
families, young people, and older residents—listening to what 
matters most to you.
I’m especially proud of helping raise funds for the Cambridge 
Cancer Research Hospital supporting life-saving research and 
care that touches so many of our families 
 My priorities are simple:
· Stronger communities—tackling inequality, supporting local 
facilities, and creating safe, welcoming spaces.
· Homes for local people—because everyone deserves a secure 
place to live., specially our next generation, we have to build 
affordable house, I believe if we don’t do anything now then it will be 
really difficult for them to buy a house and live in Cambridge ,
· Safer roads—fixing potholes and improving transport to make daily 
life easier. That will help reduce traffic in cambridge
I want to be a visible, approachable councillor who listens and 
delivers practical solutions. From supporting young people through 
sport,, I believe sports can bring everybody together, helping older 
residents stay connected, to tackling anti-social behaviour, I’ll work 
with you to get results.
I’m committed to making sure your voices are heard in every 
decision that affects our community. I ask for your support, so 
together we can build a safer, fairer, and stronger Queen Edith’s.

Farrell is a strong community campaigner and will bring a fresh energy, a stronger voice to residents who have been overlooked by Camden Labour. As a committee member that organises the annual Jester Festival on Fortune Green, a member of Friends of Fortune Green and the West Hampstead Amenity and Transport Group, Farrell is working alongside Cllr Nancy Jirira to improve everyday life in Fortune Green.
He is passionate about social mobility, having spent his career working with charities to help young people reach their potential. He is a Trustee and Treasurer of the Magic Circle Foundation, as well as works on a cross-party basis to tackle the rise of far-right extremism and has served on the boards of several charities, giving him a strong track record of responsibility and ensuring organisations work for the people they serve, driven by a commitment to fairness.

I am proud to call Camden my home, especially because I grew up facing limited opportunities. My parents have always worked incredibly hard as responsible citizens, even though their efforts often went unnoticed. Their dedication truly inspires me, and I am grateful for the community that surrounds us.

But over the years, I have seen the growing gap between us —the residents—and the Labour-led council in Camden. With rising crime, overdevelopment, unsafe neighbourhoods, and antisocial behaviour, there is a real sense of mistrust between us and the council. Is Camden listening? No!

These experiences have taught me that we must act to serve and protect our community, as true service comes from action, not just ambition.

This is why I am proud to be part of the Primrose Hill Community Association and The Friends of Regent’s Park and Primrose Hill, committed to supporting residents—especially the vulnerable—restoring residents’ voices, and revitalising our community. As a Local School Governor, I engage with parents, staff, and the wider community to ensure that our pupils achieve the best educational standards. By participating in the Primrose Hill Safer Neighbourhoods Team (PHSNT) panel meetings, I share consistent and timely information with the police, helping to foster a safer community and maintain a high quality of life for residents in Primrose Hill.

My focus is on ensuring we have clean and safer neighbourhoods. We need a visible police presence, more CCTV coverage, and increased street lighting to help us feel safe in Primrose Hill. As your prospective Councillor, my priority will be to fight for Primrose Hill to receive its fair share of community neighbourhood policing from the Metropolitan Police. I also aim to restore due diligence in development and consultation processes by promoting in-person consultations instead of relying on online robots, to help rebuild trust between residents and the council. Primrose Hill residents must be heard before final development decisions are made.

I am standing as your prospective Councillor to listen, bridge the gap between us and Camden, ensure our voices are heard, prevent concerns from being overlooked, and keep the Primrose Hill dream alive.

statement_to_voters:
Aarti Wadhwani grew up in Golders Green and attended Channing School in Highgate, before graduating and finance from university.
Aarti has been living in West Hampstead for the past five years and works in Recruitment for an asset management company.
Aarti is a committed community leader, fundraiser and volunteers for Sadhu Vaswani UK, a charity promoting vegetarianism and holds weekly distribution events to feed the homeless in Holborn.
As someone active in our local community, Aarti is keen to support activities for local elderly residents, wants to improve public safety and is very interested in maintaining her local area when it comes to the council achieving value for money, when the council spends our council tax.

Our ward contains some of the most vital infrastructure in Banbury, yet we never seem to be prioritised, a vote for me will help me change that.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

Bryonie has lived locally in Bicester all her life and works as a carer in the community, giving her an understanding of the challenges local people face. Bryonie wants to represent Bicester East with the passion and energy residents deserve.
Julius has spent his career delivering results from people who need help from the local council. This means he has the right skills and experience to make sure residents get the support they need.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres

I am active campaigner in Chorley South. I am always out and about,
speaking to residents and working hard on the things they tell me they
care most about. I have had significant success in ensuring improvements
to our streets and pavements.

I live in Chorley with my husband. I have been teaching languages and
employability skills to young people across Lancashire for almost 30
years, and leading on big-scale higher education projects. I speak
fluent Spanish and French, and in my free time enjoy gardening, walking
in our beautiful local countryside as well as working with the NHS and other
organisations on techniques for good mental health.

For the past five years, I have been an active member of the Chorley Green Party team, working hard to get the best for our amazing town.

I’m Tramore (but I go by Tram) and I’ve lived and worked in Coventry for around 8 years now with my Husband and foster son and I work for Coventry University.

I’m very proud of the community I live in and of the diversity which makes Coventry such a wonderful city.

My priorities are will be to ensure that local issues are dealt with promptly and residents concerns are not only heard, but actioned. I’ve seen this spirit from Green Party councillors in Coventry: they’re a visible and constant presence all year round, not just during election time. This type of community engagement is vital to improving our areas.

I am a dedicated community-focused individual with a passion for fairness, inclusion, and sustainable development. I believe in practical solutions that improve everyday life for local residents.
I have been part of the Coventry community for several years. I value its diversity, strong community spirit, and rich history, which make it a unique and welcoming place to live and grow.
My priorities are improving local services, supporting small businesses, tackling the cost-of-living crisis, and promoting cleaner, greener neighbourhoods. I will work to ensure every voice is heard and no community is left behind.

I’m Sujata Misala, a passionate community member dedicated to supporting local people and creating positive change. I believe in fairness, inclusion, and building opportunities for everyone.
I have lived in Coventry for several years. It’s a diverse and welcoming city where communities come together. I appreciate its strong sense of unity, culture, and shared support.
My priorities are improving housing, supporting small businesses, and keeping our neighbourhoods clean and safe. I want to listen to residents, strengthen local services, and ensure equal opportunities for all.

I am Kolapo Abubakar, an experienced Specification Technologist with over 15 years in the food industry, a University of Salford alumnus, and a proud resident of Longford, Coventry.

As a Green Party candidate for Longford Ward Councillor, I am committed to honest representation and practical leadership that reflects residents’ needs.

My priorities are cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, affordable housing, better local services, and strong support for green and sustainable initiatives, working closely with residents and community groups to make Longford a better and more inclusive place to live and work.

Kirthika is a forensic science graduate who after working in the field for a few years, made the leap to healthcare in order to have a more meaningful impact on people’s lives.

She joined the Green party for the same reason, wanting to uplift those in her community and create a better life for everyone.

In her spare time she can be found reading high fantasy books and working on her current crochet project.

statement_to_voters:
Titilope moved to Croydon with her family in 2016. She Holds B.Eng. Chemical Engineering, Certificate in Formulating Project Proposal for Climate Change Mitigation & Clean Energy, Certificate in Sustainable Environmental Management, Level II Domestic Retrofit, and Level 3 Energy Awareness.

She is a certified engineer and experienced Environmental Manager with 12 years of experience in climate change, renewable energy, emission management, and sustainable development working in the public sector.

Presently she is an Energy Advisor in the community sector because she is passionate about ensuring people are at the centre of the energy transition and believes that climate change and fuel poverty are both social justice issues and that they must be tackled together.

Titilope's priorities for Woodside include:

* ensuring residents get value for Council Tax paid; * promoting local job creation by encouraging apprenticeships in green jobs; * working with the Safer Neighborhood Team to make Woodside a safe place to live in; * promoting and helping build the confidence of young girls by promoting empowerment programs, seminars, and training in schools.

I am standing to be a candidate in the wonderful Crystal Palace & Upper Norwood ward not for personal gain or kudos, not for political aspirations, but from a strong desire to make a real difference to the community.

For 11 years I was on the committee of the local Marie Curie Charity and then for 4 years I was Chairman of the RSPCA in South London

I designed and still run the websites for our church on Westow Street and a local Labour Party

Now my focus is on helping residents, community groups & businesses to deal with their issues, champion their ideas and alleviate their concerns.

Recently I have had many dozens of fly-tips removed, have secured funding for the paths of Upper Norwood Rec to be repaired, started a big campaign to get Croydon Council to clean up & maintain Beulah Hill Pond and another to clear the horrid fly-tip behind Victory Place off the Triangle.

I have also helped multiple residents have issues fixed in their homes & on their streets.

After a long career in finance & payments, plus expertise in charity & institution governance, I am now semi-retired and ready to bring my business acumen and expert organisational skills to give back to a community I love.

I am here to serve you, not inflate my ego or impress my friends.

I’m standing to be a councillor who’s visible, approachable, and firmly on the side of local people.

Crystal Palace & Upper Norwood has a unique, independent spirit — and I want to protect and strengthen that. From supporting our brilliant high streets and local businesses to keeping our streets clean and safe, I’ll focus on the things that matter day to day.

I believe good councillors listen and act. That means being out in the community, taking concerns seriously, and following through to get results.

Working with a Labour government, we have a real opportunity to bring more investment into our area — and I’ll make sure our community benefits.

If elected, I’ll be a strong voice for residents and a councillor you can rely on.

I am standing for Reform UK because I believe Croydon’s future depends on practical leadership, financial responsibility, and restoring trust in local government. Having lived in the borough for many years, I’ve seen first-hand the challenges residents face — from high council tax to declining public services — and I want to be part of the solution. My goal is to ensure that every pound spent by the council delivers real value to our community.

Professionally, I work in technology and data-driven problem-solving, where clarity, accountability, and results matter. These are values I want to bring into local decision-making. I am committed to cutting waste, improving service delivery, and championing a more transparent, resident-focused council. Croydon deserves strong, competent representation, and I want to use my experience and determination to deliver meaningful improvements for Fairfield and our wider community.

I stood to be a councillor in 2022 because I was fed up of seeing systems fail the people who need them most. But over the last 4 years I’ve seen just how deep those failures go. There’s been far too many ways the council has failed people, sometimes with devastating impact.

I care deeply about people - whether I represent you, or not; whether you vote for me or not - and I work hard, sometimes fiercely advocating for residents and community organisations. But I shouldn’t have to - at least not for residents to have the bare minimum: communication.

Politicians need to dream bigger for Croydon, but that vision should start with getting the basics right at the council first. I want Croydon to have a caring council, and one that that puts power back into residents’ hands. I know only the Greens will deliver that.

Both Labour and the Conservatives have failed Croydon, bankrupting the council, having poor management and making bad decisions. Now bills continue to rise, and residents are paying 33% more in council tax, for less services – not even remotely touching the council's debt. Greens have consistently voted against council tax increases, and put forward budget amendments showing that some services didn’t need to be cut. We’ve advocated that the government needs to intervene when it comes to Croydon's debt – and now have the establishment parties agreeing.

Croydon is a borough with so much potential. I was born and raised in Croydon, and I live in Fairfield. I see the issues in the town centre every day, and I’m determined to bring it back to life and tidy up our neighbourhoods.

As a councillor I’ve enjoyed working alongside residents and campaigners to get their issues heard and resolved, particularly on housing, equality and environmental issues. You can find out more about what we’ve delivered on https://croydon.greenparty.org.uk/fairfield/ We listen to residents and actually take action.

With your vote and with more Greens, we can get more done – and there’s a lot to do. Different choices can be made: fairer, more compassionate ones, with residents. Vote Green.

I live in Kenley with my wife Fiona and our two-year-old daughter Chloe. We love Kenley and have chosen to make it our home for our young family.

I’ve been a committed community activist for my entire working life. I’m proud to have spent a large amount of my career leading with lots of different groups to promote community cohesion, particularly among young people. For me, nothing is more important than building a strong, passionate community. If I’m lucky enough to be elected as the next Councillor for Kenley, that’s what you’ll get from me 24 hours a day.

That’s what I think Kenley needs. We’re full of so many incredible local groups run by committed local people. I want to work with you all to ensure you get the support that you need from Croydon Council.

I am a proud New Addington resident and Community Advocate.
I have lived here for many years, working closely with local families and understanding the issues that matter in most to residents.
I am standing to champion Safer Streets, Stronger Services and a brighter future for New Addington.
I promise to put People First.
I am thrilled that this Labour government has awarded New Addington £20m Pride in Place funding. I will work with the Independent Panel and people from across New Addington to ensure that Residents’ priorities are met and that this investment delivers real improvements for everyone.
Croydon is becoming a ghost town but you can help us save it by voting all Labour and Cooperative candidates on 7th May.

party_description_text: Taking the Initiative Party of Britain

statement_to_voters: Kelsey is a first year student at the University of Oxford, and has a background in environmental and social justice campaigning. Kelsey is passionate about ensuring that Oxford is a city with a strong and thriving local democracy that places people and the planet are put first. As a disabled young person, Kelsey is dedicated to seeing inclusion and representation at all levels.

party_description_text: Taking the Initiative Party of Britain
Taking the Initiative Party of Britain

statement_to_voters:
- Have lived in Norbury for over 15 years 
- Local mother 
- Advocate for fair accessible SEND and alternative educational provisions
- Adult and Paediatric Special Care Dentist in the NHS for 20 years
- Previously a University of London Lecturer
- Anti-racist/anti-colonial/anti-imperialist

I am standing because I believe in **People Over Profit**.
Its time we stop looking to billionaire corporates to solve our housing, health, energy and mental health crisis; they have been the beneficiaries of a system tipped against ordinary working people for too long.
**For true _people-led transparent democracy_, invested in local community building, where our children and grandchildren have a safe. clean and positive future, VOTE GREEN**

I am a legal immigrant, a classical liberal, and a patriot by choice who believes that Britain’s strength lies in its values, not its identity politics. My career in the technology sector has been dedicated to one thing: efficiency. As an Agile Programme Manager and practitioner of Lean Six Sigma, I specialise in optimising complex systems and ruthlessly eliminating waste.

I apply that same analytical rigour to politics. In Reform, I see a movement demanding common sense. They are the only party brave enough to judge individuals by the content of their character rather than the colour of their skin or the quotas they fill. My own journey to this country was driven by a respect for British law and liberty—ideals that are currently threatened by weak borders, wasteful spending, and a policing system afraid to enforce order.

I stand for an energy policy that prioritizes our sovereignty over expensive dogma, a tax system that rewards hard work rather than punishing success, and a return to focused, impartial policing. We must reject the soft bigotry of stereotyping and embrace a fierce meritocracy where freedom and security go hand in hand. I am here to fight for a future where this country is safe, prosperous, and unapologetically free.

party_description_text: Taking the Initiative Party of Britain
Taking the Initiative Party of Britain
Taking the Initiative Party of Britain
Taking the Initiative Party of Britain
Taking the Initiative Party of Britain

statement_to_voters:
Dr Munir Abbasi is Labour’s candidate for Central Greenford in the 2026 London Borough of Ealing council elections. A proud resident of Greenford, he is committed to serving the local community and ensuring that residents’ voices are represented in local decision-making using bottom-up approach.

Dr Abbasi holds a PhD in engineering and is a professional engineer. His work focuses on delivering practical solutions that support resilient communities, social innovation, advance climate-neutral, and sustainable development.

Alongside his professional career, Dr Abbasi has been actively involved in community service. He is serving as a school governor, trustee of the hub@Greenford Library, and previously sat on the Ealing Citizens’ Equality Tribunal, helping to address inequality and promote fairness across the borough.
He is passionate about strong public services, safer neighbourhoods, cleaner streets and parks, opportunities for young people, and fair development that benefits local residents.
 Dr Abbasi is committed to working with the community to help make Central Greenford a cleaner, safer, and more prosperous place for everyone.

party_description_text: Ealing Community Independents - Putting Southall First
statement_to_voters: Raj has lived in Ealing for 39 years and worked in the transport logistics, manufacturing and security industries. He has been a union rep for two unions. Raj has supported local campaigns, concluding those to save accident and emergency services and keep the maternity unit open at Ealing hospital, keep Southall Town Hall open and keep a factory open in North Ealing that employs disabled people. He has previously stood as a council candidate for Ealing in Hanger hill, and as a parliamentary candidate in Hammersmith and Chiswick. Living in the area with his family, Raj understands the everyday concerns facing residents. He is standing to represent our community with integrity, listen to your concerns, and work collaboratively to deliver real improvements.
Lakhbir Singh, former Mayor of Luton, regularly uncovers and reports fly tips in Ealing Common. He also volunteers for LAGER Can, to keep our streets and parks clean.

Abdi Ahmed is a lifelong Ealing Borough resident. He works for a Liberal Democrat Member of Parliament as a Parliamentary Researcher, focusing on issues such as local government finance, public services, and cost-of-living pressures. Abdi previously worked on a £48 million UK Foreign Office programme supporting greener, more resilient cities. He is a fluent Somali speaker, able to engage and represent East Acton's diverse communities, and is experienced in dealing with policy casework in Parliament, helping residents get answers and solutions.

My name is Hadi, and I am honoured to be standing as a candidate to represent our community.

Having grown up in Hanger Hill, I have developed a strong appreciation for our area and the people who make it what it is. I have been actively involved in volunteering within our community, helping care for local parks and protect the green spaces that residents value. I also lead weekly food drives in Acton and Westminster, organising volunteers and providing hot food and drinks to those in need. Through this work, I have developed a strong sense of responsibility, teamwork, and a commitment to standing up for those whose voices are often overlooked.

I am passionate about serving our community with integrity, ensuring residents feel represented, and helping to build a fairer, more supportive local area. I believe effective representation begins with listening, and I am committed to being an approachable, reliable, and dedicated voice for our community.

I am currently a Chemical Engineering student and Course Representative, where I work closely with students and staff to ensure voices are heard and meaningful improvements are made. This role has strengthened my commitment to listening, advocating, and delivering practical solutions.

I’m proud to be standing in North Greenford as your Green Party candidate. Living locally in Ealing, I’m committed to working with residents to build a fairer, more sustainable community that reflects your priorities.

Local politics should be about listening properly and taking action for cleaner, safer streets, stronger local services, and a council that’s open, responsive, and accountable every month, not just at election time.

Alongside my community focus, I bring experience from the infrastructure and construction industry, where I negotiate contracts and advocate for proper scrutiny of how public funds – raised from your hard‑earned money – are spent. I’ve also led diversity and inclusion work, seeing both the positive impact it can have and how easily progress can be rolled back when the wider political climate shifts. That’s why lasting change must be rooted in communities and carried through with consistency.

Politics works best when it’s grounded in real‑life experience. We need more people to step forward, serve their communities for a period of time, and bring practical skills into decision‑making – rather than treating politics as a lifelong career.

From Sudbury Hill to Horsenden, I’ll stand up for residents across North Greenford all year round – focused on delivering a greener, fairer borough that works for all of us.

party_description_text: Ealing Community Independents - Putting Southall First
statement_to_voters: Jahangir is a technology professional with over 25 years’ experience in technology, research, and enterprise leadership. He currently leads analytics and digital transformation projects within an international organisation. He has also served in policy and non-profit roles supporting sectoral development and child education initiatives.  He is standing in Norwood Green because he believes stronger local leadership is needed to prevent the rise of far right driven by wrong Labour policies and their overall poor economic performance. His priorities would also be about addressing deteriorating living standards in the ward and tackling youth joblessness. He wants to revitalise Southall’s local businesses and create real employment pathways for residents in a meaningful way.

In 2025, I was elected to serve on the Green Party’s Policy Development Committee, where I work to develop practical, forward-looking policies. I am an enthusiastic academic, campaigner, and policy developer dedicated to making a positive impact. My education and research at leading UK universities including Oxford and Cambridge has focused on how institutions can become more accountable, effective, and responsive to public needs.

Ealing has been my home for 15 years, I understand the challenges our residents face, from rising rents and housing costs to overdevelopment without sufficient infrastructure, air and water quality concerns, stretched local services, and limited opportunities for our young people.

Many people feel the system is not working for them. Too often, decisions are made without proper scrutiny or meaningful consultation. Residents want genuinely affordable housing, clean streets, maintained green spaces, safe neighbourhoods, well-supported schools, and transparency in how taxpayers’ money is spent. Managing decline is not leadership. We need ambition and accountability.

In 2022, the Green Party came second in this ward. Since then, we have grown strongly because more people recognise that real change is needed. We are now the credible alternative for those seeking hope, integrity, long-term thinking, and practical solutions.

If we want meaningful change in Ealing, we need Green councillors demanding social justice, environmental responsibility and prioritising people.

I will bring openness, scrutiny and evidence-based decision-making to South Acton and contribute to the wider Ealing alongside other Green elected councillors.

I will listen carefully, and work tirelessly to make sure that local decisions genuinely reflect what our community needs.

Please support me in South Acton and kindly encourage neighbours across Ealing to back Green candidates. Every conversation is valuable, and every vote truly makes a difference.

As your councillor, I will bring openness, scrutiny, transparency and evidence-based decision-making to South Acton and make a meaningful contribution to the wider Ealing alongside other Green elected councillors.

I will listen carefully, offer constructive challenges, and work tirelessly to make sure that local decisions genuinely reflect what our community needs.

Please support me in South Acton and kindly encourage friends and neighbours across all other Ealing Wards to back Green candidates. Every conversation is valuable, and every vote truly makes a difference.

Let us shift from frustration to action and work together to create the fairer, greener Ealing we all deserve.

I’ve always cared somewhat about the natural world, and then I realised our environment is our life support system and needs to be regenerated so people can live full, healthy and happy lives. Therefore I joined the Green Party as they are the only ones prioritising our health and economic competitiveness.
Only the Greens have ambitious plans for education, which is the most efficient way to create real equality in our society, and a stronger economy. This, on top of building high quality low energy homes to permanently reduce the cost of living, means the Green Party is focussed on helping people to thrive, instead of just getting by.

Bors has trained as a social scientist and a climate scientist. His expert area is climate migration.
He runs a small research business within healthcare.

His evening job is climate activism.

He is Chair of the Sustainability Committee at the British Healthcare Business Intelligence Association. 
He regularly gives talks about the climate emergency to audiences across the South.

Bors has lived in Chandler's Ford for over 10 years, is an active member of the community and a scout leader.

Bors has 3 boys, all attending local schools. His vision is to leave a fair and liveable planet to future generations.

Prad was one of the leading voices at the Planning Appeal against the County Council’s backing of Cemex’s plan for gravel extraction at Hamble Airfield and 15 years of additional heavy traffic on Hamble Lane. He is continuing his opposition with the support of the community campaign group, Hamble Parish Council and the area’s County Councillor, Cllr Keith House. Prad is backing concerns in Netley from residents worried by speeding traffic through the village and helped secure a reduced speed limit at Grange Road near the Primary School entrance. He is Cabinet Member for Children’s Services at the Borough Council.

Having joined the Lib Dems whilst studying politics at Reigate College, Trixie is keen to get further involved in local politics, helping the people of Banstead, Woodmansterne and Chipstead. Being a Young Liberal candidate, Trixie is perfectly placed to work with all generations to improve East Surrey. As well as being an active campaigner, Trixie is working towards a BSc in Natural Sciences.

I am a comedy writer, performer and producer for television and radio. I have written for Have I Got News For You, The News Quiz, Gigglebiz, have been on two BAFTA-winning writing teams and I was Head Writer on Bad Dinosaurs for Netflix. I have lived in Dorking and East Surrey for twenty years and have brought my children up in the local schools (North Downs Primary and The Ashcombe). Both my father and grandfather were ministers at Leatherhead United Reformed Church. I am a company founder, and an Associate Fellow of the Royal Historical Society. I regularly perform at Comedy At The Cottage in Redhill, and have spent a lot of time playing fake politicians at fundraisers and on Channel 4’s Tonightly. I currently run an animation production company, and live with his wife, children, dog, and cat.

Neha has lived in Salfords since 2012, and locally in Reigate and Banstead since 2004 with her family. Neha started and leads the Asian Social Group which hosts community events and sports activities to help build community cohesion. After a career working in finance Neha has run her own business in Horley since 2016. Neha says, “I hope to make a difference helping residents access council services and address issues that affect their everyday lives – no matter how small.”

With over 40 years of dedicated service as an NHS manager and agency lead, I bring a lifetime of leadership, compassion, and practical problem-solving experience to our community. Now a retired 75-year-old, I remain deeply active within my political party and find great fulfillment in volunteering for several local charities. My career has been defined by a commitment to public service and the well-being of others, qualities I am eager to channel into representing our residents with the same kindness, politeness, and friendly approachable nature that I have carried throughout my professional life.
 
On a personal note, I am the proud father of two children and grandfather of five grandchildren and am inspired daily by my wife, a University lecturer with a doctorate in Nursing. Our family’s deep roots in healthcare and education reinforce my belief in the power of community support and lifelong learning. I am running for councillor because I believe my extensive experience in management and my heart for charitable work make me uniquely qualified to listen to your concerns and advocate for a better, more inclusive future for everyone in our ward.

I’ve grown up in Epsom and Ewell and feel fiercely protective of this neighbourhood. Its beautiful parks, charming town centre and phenomenal schools are reasons I chose to keep my roots here. I would work hard for the people of this ward as councillor. I am a strong believer that if you raise up the marginalised in society, we all rise up with them. I have studied law and worked to provide legal aid to asylum seekers. I currently work within a trade union. I would bring the compassion and intelligence required in this line of work to advocate for each and every one of our neighbours.

**Vote Steel and Victoria and make hope normal again**

I’m Steel Horton, I live in Langshott and I’m standing for you in the new East Surrey Council because I believe Horley and our wider community deserve leaders who listen, who act with integrity, and who are ready to get stuck in to solve the real problems we face every day.

For too long, we’ve seen neglect, empty promises, and a system that feels disconnected from our needs. Whether it’s crumbling roads, unsafe streets, a lack of affordable homes, or the urgent climate crisis, we know that change is possible, but it requires experience, courage, and a genuine commitment to our local area.

As your Green Party candidate, I’ll work to deliver the change that we need. My vision is simple: a place where every resident has access to the services, infrastructure, and financial stability they need to thrive.

**Here is what Victoria and I will fight to achieve if elected:**

- **Fix Our Roads and Make Streets Safer:** I will push for better maintenance, introduce 20mph zones around schools, and expand safe cycling and walking routes to protect our families.
- **Build Genuinely Affordable Homes:** I will prioritise socially rented housing to address waiting lists while fiercely protecting our Green Belt and ensuring new developments come with the schools, doctors, and transport we actually need.
- **Lead on Climate Action:** From retrofitting homes to lower energy bills to decarbonising council operations by 2030, I will make climate action the heart of every decision.
- **Invest in Our Families and Vulnerable Residents:** I will work to expand children’s centres, improve mental health support in schools, and ensure our adult social care is person-centred and dignified.
- **Bring Services Back In-House:** I believe in holding power to account. I will work to bring key services like waste management and bus operations back under local control to improve quality and accountability.
- **Divest and Invest Ethically:** I will push to divest council pensions from fossil fuels and invest in sustainable alternatives that benefit our local economy.
- **Revitalise Our Communities:** I will support the reopening of arts venues, invest in sports facilities, and use vacant high street spaces for community purposes to bring people together.

My colleague Victoria and I are ready to work hard for Horley. We are not afraid of the tough jobs, and we won’t be silenced by those in power. We believe in fairness, honesty, and hope.

Let’s build a safer, greener, and kinder Horley together.

**Vote Steel and Victoria and make hope normal again.**

As your councillor I will fight tirelessly for better maintenance of our roads, road improvement and much better facilities for cyclists and pedestrians. I will also fight to raise the standard of social care and other services. And all this will demand better funding of the council – and I will be there to fight for that.

I joined the Green Party because environmentalism has been a part of my life since primary school all the way up to my adult life. I still remember the buzz of the Eco-Schools meetings and how that early hands-on experience, from energy monitoring to school garden projects, stayed with me all the way through secondary school. It really shaped how I see the world, teaching me early on that small, local changes actually lead to a much bigger impact.

I have lived in Leatherhead for the past five years and am proud to call it home. I believe our community deserves a hopeful future shaped by fresh ideas and more young voices in local politics. As a Green councillor, I would campaign to protect our green spaces, improve walking, cycling and public transport, support local businesses, and push for genuinely affordable housing. The Green Party is championing bold action to tackle both the rising cost of living and the climate crisis. I want to bring that same ambition locally – standing up for our community, the local environment and helping create a fairer, greener future for Leatherhead.

Fredericka is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Oxted and has lived locally her whole life, building strong roots in the community. She has worked at both Wetherspoons and the Co-op, giving her a clear understanding of the day-to-day experiences of local residents and the importance of reliable services and a thriving high street. She enjoys making the most of what Oxted has to offer, from visits to the cinema and swimming pool to walks in the surrounding countryside, and has a strong appreciation for what makes the area special.

Fredericka values Oxted’s unique village feel and is committed to protecting it as the area grows and changes. She believes it is essential that roads, public services and local infrastructure keep pace with new development so that Oxted remains a place where people are proud to live. Her priority is to support a strong, connected community with well-maintained infrastructure and to ensure residents have a clear voice in the decisions that affect their town.

Will has nearly 25 years' experience working in the non-profit sector, including four years at a disabled people's charity and a decade in sexual health. His voluteer roles have ranged from transcribing historical documents to marshalling at junior parkruns. He has been a trustee of a number of community organisations, including campaign groups and performing arts and local history charities.

The new unitary council offers a fresh start and a chance to deliver high quality, cost-effective public services across East Surrey, but it will also be larger than Tandridge District Council and more remote from the people it serves. Will is keen to ensure that our communities are not neglected and will fight to see our local area gets its fair share of support and investment.

I have lived in and around the area for over 50 years.  I grew up in Tadworth and am passionate that our area retains a knowledgeable, local and experienced Councillor to represent residents views accurately and without any political party bias.  A true, unbiased voice for residents.  I have represented the area as a local Councillor for the past five years and I am keen to continue protecting the individual identities of our villages.  I remain committed to representing residents in a non-party political way, bringing our communities together whilst respecting and preserving what makes each one unique.  I offer a true, unbiased voice for residents.

Sammi was raised on Heatherside Road, married just around the corner at All Saints Church, and now has children in local childcare - a true West Ewell local. Now working as a Sign Language Interpreter, Sammi is passionate about building an inclusive community that is welcoming for all, and she is particularly committed to improving SEND provision so that every child is given the opportunity to thrive. Having grown up in West Ewell herself, she understands the everyday issues that matter to local residents and actually cares about finding solutions. Sammi is motivated by a desire to show her children that fairness, sensibility and collaborative leadership can achieve real progress. When she's not working, you'll often find Sammi and her husband at Bourne Hall, keeping up with their two children and enjoying time together as a family.

I’ve been working in East Sussex, from Hastings to Eastbourne, for the last 7 and a half years, as a veterinary surgeon. I’ve also been living in Hailsham/Hellingly for the past 5 years. What I love most about our county are its people and the great natural beauty that the countryside and coastal areas provide us.

Being originally from Portugal, I am happy to call this country my home now. I’ve experienced first hand the hospitality and humanity of the British people and have nothing but good things to say about it. This comes into stark contrast with what we hear in the media. A loud minority would like us to think that the country is divided and are trying to turn neighbour against neighbour. But our day-to-day experiences show us the opposite: a country united by our shared values of peace, freedom, democracy, mutual understanding and the pursuit of happiness; not only for the few, but for all of us!

For too many years, politicians have been disconnected from these shared values. Understandably, people have become disillusioned with politics and feeling that nothing can be changed. Nonetheless, this doesn’t mean that democracy doesn’t work. It simply means that we need better politicians, who closely align with people’s shared values. I believe that the Green Party is bringing back humanity into politics, mirroring British common values.

I’m proud to be the Green Party’s County Councillor candidate for Arlington, East Hoathly & Hellingly. If elected, I will focus my energy and efforts in the following areas:

● Road maintenance: make sure that current maintenance contracts are fit for purpose, delivering good value for money and delivering good performance. I will focus on the motto that it’s better to fix something properly the first time, than multiple times in a lesser manner, to deliver long-term sustainability and reliability for our road infrastructure.

● Community and local businesses: create an online directory of community groups, volunteering opportunities and locally available jobs, so locals can easily find what’s available near them. Incentivize local shopping and support for local businesses via this directory, where businesses will be able to advertise their goods and services.

● Communication and transparency: keep voters informed about what’s happening in Council meetings and how decisions will impact them, with the possibility of direct and indirect participation, via online polls and Council open days.

I would be very pleased to count on your vote in the local elections. Vote Green to bring back trust and accountability into politics. Vote Green to bring back hope.

I have worked within private, public and academic sectors. As a postman, administrator, commercial-archaeologist, project manager, and now in IT. I've worked alongside charities and communities. I've seen the struggles people face. As a family person with children, we share concerns about worsening conditions. And we too feel the inequality led cost-of-living crisis.

As one of three Rother District Green Party councillors, we know how local government does its best in hard-times. Where we've helped balanced budgets whilst safeguarding people and nature. But only by having more Greens to replace the Conservative led County Council, can we offer real hope and make real change.

Let's have a Bexhill and East Sussex for the people and push back against austerity. Let's improve our future with practical, honest ideas just like we did post-war. Let's have similar renewal now.

NEW EAST SUSSEX UNITARY COUNCIL FORMATION

A new unitary that works for Bexhill and East Sussex's communities is needed. A unitary based on community, wellbeing, arts, culture and nature that represents the best of Bexhill, and the county we live in - East Sussex. A unitary that is not created with austerity in mind but aims to grow and cater for residents needs and aspirations.

EAST SUSSEX COUNTY COUNCIL

Hope for our young & working-age adults.

Skilled jobs, education, activities & engagement from clubs to new businesses. And protecting nature & wildlife- creating a safer Bexhill & Sussex for their future.

Review contracts for the benefit of people not profits.

Whether care, schools, NHS, highways and public transport - those in need should have help. Prioritise worse roads. Look at in-house solutions as contracts end.

Hold an emergency summit to address Tory-led austerity.

Stand against the economic policies of major parties that led to local government decline. Show there is another way. End inequality and the cost-of-living crisis!

Specific ESCC Issues

Highway Safety, Greening & layouts
There are examples where roads and streets haven’t been updated or changed in years. Since the 2022 Highway Code for example, where pedestrians and cyclist have right-of-way.
