Vote Conservative and Protect Pimlico.

Murad Gassanly, Roberto Salvia, and James Spencer
Your local Conservative candidates for Westminster's Pimlico South ward

Rajiv is the founder and director of a human rights non-profit working across the UK, where he has built a strong track record as a grassroots organiser, campaigner, and advocate. His work focuses on bringing people together to challenge injustice and push for meaningful change. Having lived in Westminster for most of his life, Rajiv has spent years engaging with residents, community organisations, and local businesses, giving him a clear understanding of the challenges people face across the borough.

He is passionate about strengthening public services and standing up for equality. He has also been an active voice in wider human rights movements, including campaigning for an end to the genocide in Palestine since October 2023. As a Green councillor, Rajiv would bring proven experience, a strong sense of justice, and a commitment to delivering for local people.

The people of Westminster deserve the best from their council. Safe streets, clean neighbourhoods, assistance for those most in need, and public spaces that make Westminster one of the finest cities in the world.

But when we talk to residents across St John’s Wood & North Marylebone, the same concerns come up time and again. Too many people tell us they don’t feel as safe as they used to. Streets are not as clean as they should be. Costs are rising while standards fall. And too often, it feels like the Labour-led council simply is not listening.

That must change.

We are re-standing as your local Conservatives in May because Westminster deserves better. A council that keeps your city safe, looks after your streets to a high standard, spends your money wisely, and listens to the people who live here.

Our plan is built around what local people have told us: safe streets with visible enforcement, clean and well-maintained public spaces, and value for money services with every pound of council tax spent on the services that really matter. No more waste, no more reckless spending.

We have the plan, the team and the commitment to deliver it. But we need your support to make it happen.

On Thursday, 7th May, you have the chance to vote for real change in Westminster. We are asking for your trust and your vote to deliver the city you deserve.

Cllr Ralu Oteh-Osoka, Cllr Robert Rigby, and Cllr Paul Swaddle
Your local Conservative candidates for Westminster's Regent's Park ward

Residents of St. James’s ward deserve a council that gets the basics right: safe streets, clean neighbourhoods, transport that works for everyone, and public spaces that make Westminster a place we can all be proud of.

But when we speak with local people, we hear the same message: too many people feel less safe than they used to, police presence is too thin on the ground, and our streets are being left looking tired. Costs are rising, yet standards are slipping, and the Labour council simply is not listening despite seemingly endless consultations.

That isn’t good enough, and it doesn’t have to be this way. That’s why we’re standing as your local Conservative candidates this May: to bring a sharper focus on safety, cleaner streets, and a council that treats your money with respect.

Our priorities are simple, and they come straight from what you’ve told us:

Safer streets, with visible local enforcement and action on anti-social behaviour.
Cleaner neighbourhoods and better-kept public spaces every day, not just for special occasions.
Value for money: every pound of council tax is focused on frontline services, not waste.
We’re ready to get to work, and we’ll keep listening all year round, not just at election time. If you back us, we’ll fight for the St. James’s you expect and deserve.

On Thursday, 7th May, please vote Conservative for real change in Westminster.

San Abishev, Cllr Tim Mitchell, and Cllr Mark Shearer
Your local Conservative candidates for Westminster's St. James's ward

Westbourne combines busy streets, local homes, and a real sense of neighbourhood life, and residents deserve a council that reflects that.

People here want to see well-kept streets, safer routes through the area, and a council that understands the pressures on local families, shops, and community spaces. Around Westbourne Grove, the local station, and the streets that connect this part of Westminster, residents expect visible care and real attention to detail.

That is what we want to deliver.

We believe Westminster should be a place where local people feel heard, public spaces are looked after properly, and money is spent with care.

Our priorities are clear: safer streets, cleaner neighbourhoods, and better value for residents.

On Thursday, 7th May, you have the chance to choose a more practical and responsive approach for Westbourne and for Westminster.

Sabrina Boni, Alex Duguid, and George Eldredge
Your local Conservative candidates for Westminster's Westbourne ward

Hemashu has lived in Carterton for over 11 years. He is a sound engineer, a Prince’s Trust business mentor and a volunteer for the NHS.
 
His main concern is the impact of climate change and the environment on our children and their children - he feels that the authorities are not serious about the urgency in tackling all our sources of greenhouse gas emissions, or protecting the environment in which we live. 
 
Hemashu is a very a strong believer in justice and equality for all.
 
If elected, Hemashu would put his weight behind increased use of electric buses and the proposed rail link from Carterton to Witney and Oxford and beyond. Improved transport links for Carterton are vital for the prosperity of Carterton. 
 
He would also concentrate upon what the County can do to enhance educational provision to help children catch up on their learning now that they are back at school - especially the young children. He would, of course, also push the Council to be more supportive of schemes to encourage the rewilding of road verges and green spaces for wildlife, and to stop routine and unnecessary use of chemicals harmful to nature. The council should also try harder to influence landowners, developers, business and communities to maximise opportunities for nature.

Manu is a local business owner and has worked as a scientist in the field of neuroscience. Manu spent much of his youth in and around Spelthorne, his father worked here as a taxi driver for several decades and growing up Manu spent a great deal of time on Church Road in Ashford – seeing first-hand how vital accessible local services are to everyday life.

Satpal works in a senior role in logistics at nearby Heathrow Airport and has lived in Ashford for many years with his family. This experience makes him well placed to understand the needs of our community. Satpal believes that Ashford has been neglected by the Conservatives for far too long and will be a fantastic local champion for our town on the new Unitary Authority.

We come from many walks of life with different life experiences and different political views, but we can all unite in our desire to keep our community strong and good to live in. We may vote for national parties in national elections, but in local government the most important thing is to have capable representatives who live locally, like Isabel and myself who are both long term residents of Egham, who know the new ward well, and care about its future.

Egham town has elected Independent Resident councillors over many years. We are your neighbours; we live here and know the place and the local issues well. We’ll work with other councillors from any political party, for whatever brings most benefit to the new Egham ward and we’ll vote independently, unwhipped, however we think best for our community. Egham is simply a better place to live for having strong, local, Independent representation. Our concern is to safeguard the new Egham ward’s interests in the future, when there’ll be fewer representatives and decisions will be made more remotely, by people who may not know Egham at all.

Individual details are below, but if elected, our shared aims will be to:
 * Foster a collaborative approach, like Isabel has done as part of the Runnymede Council Alliance at RBC. This will be essential if, as likely, no single party gets overall majority.
 * Maintain services to support people living active lives, independently within their homes, in spite of budgetary pressures on the new authority.
 * Keep strong engagement with the River Thames Scheme to maximise benefits for those in high flood-risk areas in Egham Hythe and Egham.
 * Oppose gravel extraction at Milton Park and Whitehall Farms.
 * Fight for improved pedestrian access to Runnymede meadows.
 * Embed high standards for SEND at the new council.
 * Press the new authority to take potholes seriously.
 * Support the Local Cycling and Walking Infrastructure Plan, giving more choice on how to get places, reducing congestion and being good for health.
 * Attempt to crack the Pooley Green rail crossing nightmare - we will call the council to lead a task force to get action from the rail authorities.
 * Hold regular open sessions for advice, information and sharing of views.

Siobhan and I made Egham our home in 2002, attracted by the walk-able, small town feel, fast train to Bracknell for my work and being close to family. Having historic Runnymede on the doorstep appealed greatly. Various dogs have helped us make good use of it. We love Egham, its friendliness and community spirit.

I'm passionate about democracy and the environment and am particularly keen on bicycles as a tool for getting places. My retirement has given us time to get a heat pump and solar installation and we are already seeing the huge benefits of lower bills. I’d love more people to get these benefits.

I love hearing peoples ideas and will enthusiastically push good ones regardless of origin. I'm tenacious and meticulous. All things I'm confident would be useful to me as a Councillor.

I've been a fan of the Egham Residents Association (ERA) since the first newsletters dropped through my door. I love their support for Independent councillors, free from the influence of National party politics, and I’ve helped campaign for independent candidates in previous elections. In 2024 I joined the ERA committee to manage the website, making it simpler and more useful. Planning is a key focus of the committee - I submit objections when appropriate and encourage others to do so too.

Whilst having just one, unitary, Council will have advantages, local knowledge and advocacy will become crucial to avoid Egham's interests getting lost amongst other issues for the new, large Council. I will seek out ways for local voices to get heard during the transition and in the new structure, and fight Egham Ward's corner when the new Authority takes over fully in a year's time.

My priorities, beyond our shared ones above, will be to:
 * Make our ward more human focused. Less traffic danger, noise and pollution whilst keeping traffic moving.
*  Support climate action. Often this means doing more with less, saving money.
* Prioritise affordable family housing.
* Aim for good relationships with all other Councillors. Do what we can agree on. Don’t fight empty battles that lead nowhere.
* Seek views on Egham Precinct. Any expensive upgrade should yield big benefits. I'm concerned we will lose a Council asset if it becomes privately owned, with loss of flexibility.
* Seek a constructive relationship with Royal Holloway, balancing local family and student needs.

I have huge admiration for Isabel and what she’s achieved as a Councillor, in particular, getting Council meetings live streamed, making it much easier for residents and indeed Councillors to keep an eye on what is happening

Karin is a hard-working local champion who
has represented Virginia Water as a Borough
Councillor since 2024.
Karin has experience in local government
and is deeply engaged in the community. As a
councillor she has championed nature, green
spaces and sustainable modes of travel.
She has successfully campaigned on local issues and made a
difference to save the local library, fixing station lifts, investing in
playgrounds and protecting our green spaces. Karin is
committed to putting residents needs first and ensuring the new
council provides reliable, value-for-money services.

Michaela has called Farnham home for over 30 years, raising her family here and working as an independent local retailer.

She has spent her time as a Councillor working with residents, schools and community groups to secure funding, support local initiatives and strengthen the places people rely on every day. Her work has helped revitalise much loved community assets such as Bourne Pavilion, where she supported improvements to make it a more usable space for clubs and classes, and Brambleton Hall, helping fund essential refurbishment so it can continue hosting everything from toddler groups to community meetings.

She also backed the creation of new youth activity space at the Jubilee Hub, giving young people a safe, welcoming place to meet. Michaela has supported upgrades at St Peter's Primary School, including the Daily Mile track, and improvements at Rowledge Cricket Club, helping ensure these facilities remain central to local life. These are places residents use daily - where children learn, clubs gather and the community comes together.

Her priorities for the new West Surrey Council are:
• Securing funding for projects that strengthen Farnham
• Improving safety, accessibility and public spaces
• Supporting a thriving, sustainable local economy

Hello, I’m Tahir Ahmad, and I’m standing as your Green Party candidate for the Frimley Green and Mytchett ward.

I’m standing because I believe politics should be about genuine servitude and being just to people. I, as a practice, strongly believe in establishing accessible mechanisms to listen carefully to residents' needs and issues, and subsequently working hard to improve everyday life in our community.

My priorities are simple and rooted in the issues I hear about most from local families.

* Supporting **underprivileged residents** who are struggling with the rising cost of living
* Creating **better opportunities for young people**, particularly tackling youth unemployment and helping them build a positive future
* Ensuring **children with SEND receive the support they deserve**, so that families are not left fighting the system alone
* Improving **care and dignity for the elderly**, making sure older residents can live safely and comfortably in our community

I have spent much of my life working with charities and community organisations, and I care deeply about fairness, compassion, and practical solutions that improve people’s lives.

If elected, I will work hard to ensure our ward:

* Protects and strengthens its **local community spirit**
* Supports **families facing financial pressure**
* Gives **young people real opportunities**
* Provides **proper support for vulnerable residents**

As part of the Green Party, I will listen, represent your concerns honestly, and do my best to make Frimley Green and Mytchett an even better place to live for all of us.

I would be honoured to have your support.

Samar Chaudhary has lived in Goldsworth Park over the past 6 years and works as an electrician locally. He is passionate about helping Goldsworth Park residents, especially in relation to campaigning against cuts to vital public services such as education and health and in working to improve the local infrastructure.

I’ve lived here with my family for over two decades and care deeply about our community. Volunteering at Cobham Area Foodbank during Covid showed me how much a strong, connected community matters when things get tough. I have organised community events, run small businesses, supported local projects, and I remain hopeful for the future of our area.

party_name: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party
statement_to_voters:
Why am I here? What are my policies? Why should you vote for me?
These are all good questions. They are left as an exercise for the reader.

Let's think about you, the voter reading this. You're the sort of person who looks at these election nerd websites in an attempt to find out more about the people you're going to be voting for. You know I'm standing as a Monster Raving Loony; you can probably figure the rest out.

**My main campaign promises:**
1. Build a great big glorious iron curtain between West Surrey and East Surrey, to protect us from the inferior East (Wesside is de best!)
2. West Surrey will immediately launch a surprise pre-emptive strike against East Surrey. West Surrey must take the first napkin, and assert geopolitical dominance.
3. Rename "Staines-upon-Thames" back to "Staines".
4. More funding for our libraries. They do a good job.
5. Tackle the council's debt by gambling it all away at Admiral Casino on the high street.
6. Ensure that any lingering debts from Spelthorne Council are safely offloaded onto the West Surrey budget, and then enact a surprise referendum on Middlesexit. We will re-establish Middlesex!
7. Rename "Staines" back to "Staines-upon-Thames".
8. Promote more car usage (free parking in all car parks, unpedestrianize Staines High Street, more roads, fewer pavements etc) in order to solve our pothole problem (we do not have enough potholes, we need to increase the rate at which we erode the roads so we can get even more of them (potholes will continue until morale improves)).
9. Save taxpayer money on expensive proprietary software licenses by migrating the council's administrative backend computer systems to TempleOS.
10. Rename "Staines-upon-Thames" back to "Ad Pontes".

There will be more common-sense uncontroversial policies as well. You have been warned.

So, what are you waiting for? Go and register to vote (and pester everyone you know to register to vote) otherwise I'll be the only person who shows up to vote and I'll end up voting for myself and then you'll have to deal with me for the next five years which would be funny i think but also awkward for everyone.

(honestly now that I think about it, this may not necessarily be the best avenue for the application of malarkey... oh well!)

As a young adult from Staines, a recent graduate and now working for the NHS, Harleen recognises the unique challenges faced by residents of all generations. She is standing to be a dedicated advocate for Staines South and Ashford West, to prove that our voices matter, our future is exciting and that we all feel empowered, excited and genuinely connected to our community's future. Harleen will fight for opportunities and support to turn our shared concerns into tangible action for our local area.

Malgorzata has practical experience of leadership and responsibility. She currently serves as Chair of Trustees for a Polish school, where she oversees governance, works with families, and helps ensure the school is run transparently and effectively. As a first-time candidate, she wants to bring this hands-on experience into local government and be a focused, reliable voice for residents.

Ged was educated in North Wales and went on to read Economics at the University of Greenwich in London and has lived with his wife and three sons in Hindhead for over 20 years. Ged is a former Deputy Leader of Waverley Borough Council and former President of Haslemere Community Rugby Club.

From a modest background of being a builder’s son, Ged is accustomed to hard work and delivery. Ged's political views are centre-right believing in low taxation, supporting business and entrepreneurship, whilst ensuring public services are fit for purpose and deliver value for money.

Ged has been self-employed for over ten years, working as a consultant for banks and finance houses in London and the Home Counties. Ged helped set up the Haslemere Youth Hub to improve services for children and young people in his home town and now sits as a trustee on the charity board.

Ged’s decision to stand for Reform is based on a belief that a new, voter-focused approach is needed and that that the main political parties have deserted their founding principles.  Ged’s key priorities are Jobs, Education and Healthcare.

Tahir Aziz lives and works in Woking, where he runs his own mortgage advice and property management company. He is an experienced councillor, having worked for residents for seven years and Leader of the Labour Group in Woking Borough Council. As a member of the Planning Committee, he has consistently stood up for the rights of residents, campaigning for affordable homes and against overdevelopment of the town centre. He has also proposed reform of Woking Borough Council’s debt collection practices, campaigning for reduced use of bailiffs.

Cllr Anila Javaid is Labour’s candidate for Woking North and a Borough Councillor for Canalside. She is Chairman of Woking Labour and has spent over 20 years building and leading 7tnine Developments, a UK-based consultancy delivering business innovation, transformation, and performance improvement across the UK and internationally.
Anila brings a practical, evidence-led approach to public service: clear priorities, budget discipline, and a focus on outcomes residents can actually feel. Since being elected in 2023, she has supported hundreds of resident cases, working with residents, officers, and community partners to resolve problems and improve local services.
Her priorities are rooted in a simple principle: a fair deal for Woking. That means stronger negotiation on behalf of residents, better scrutiny of proposals, and transparent decision-making — particularly in areas where contracts and delivery arrangements shape everyday life, including waste and street services, housing management, regeneration programmes, and the IT systems behind access to support.
As Woking moves into a new unitary authority, Anila believes it is more important than ever to have representatives who will show up, ask the hard questions, and insist on measurable performance from delivery partners. Her commitment is to listen, act, and deliver — with integrity, accountability, and public money treated as precious.
Voice of you

Antoine was born in France but moved to the UK in 2013, he recently became a British citizen and now lives in Southbourne.

He is a cabinet maker (and amateur gardener) who cares about the environment and the impact of climate change on the local community.

One of his main concerns is the number of new houses built without adequate access to local infrastructure, forcing residents to drive everywhere to get to school, shops or GP surgeries and contributing to congestion on the road. He is also concerned about sewage discharge in our waterways, and the levels of water abstraction from local rivers such as the Ems leading to wildlife loss.

Jez  Sugars has lived in the Chichester area for eighteen years. Seven of those years in Fishbourne I trained in Bournemouth as a photographer at the Art institute, I have been in the photographic industry my whole adult life. I’m a practising Buddhist and a plant based environmentally conscious lover of nature with a keen interest in politics and fine art.

I’m proud to stand for Arborfield and Barkham ward, where I’m living for the last 6 years. A community where families, young people, and nature all deserve to thrive.

As someone passionate about protecting our environment and supporting future generations, I want to help create a safer, greener, and more accessible place for everyone, especially our pupils.

If elected as your Green Councillor, I will focus on:

**A greener future for our children:**
– Protecting local green spaces from overdevelopment
– Planting more trees and creating wildlife-friendly areas.
– Expanding no-mow verges and wildflower spaces near schools
– Reducing waste and boosting recycling across the ward

**Safer streets for pupils and families:**
– Introducing 20mph zones around schools and residential areas
– Creating safer crossings and better walking routes to school
– Supporting traffic-free or low-traffic zones at school drop-off times
– Tackling air pollution to ensure cleaner, healthier journeys

**Better, accessible transport:**
– Improving cycle lanes and safe bike storage for students
– Pushing for more reliable and affordable public transport
– Expanding electric vehicle charging while prioritising active travel

**Supporting wellbeing and opportunity:**
– Championing mental health support for young people
– Ensuring safe, inclusive community spaces for all ages
– Advocating for fair funding for local services and schools

Together, we can build an Arborfield and Barkham that puts people, planet, and future generations first.

Born in RBH and raised in Woodley, I moved to the Finchampstead area in 2010 and while my children attended Gorse Ride School I was Chair of the PTA, raising £20k each year I was in office. In 2020 I received a Civic Award for my community work running a youth club at the FBC Centre with my husband, Martin and volunteering for Finchampstead based domestic abuse charity, Kaleidoscopic UK.

Having been a Link visitor with Link Visiting Scheme for a decade, I set up the Friendship Table that meets weekly for isolated, older people and ran the FBC community team as a volunteer for 7 years for which I was awarded the Platinum Jubilee Award by Finchampstead Parish Council. During this period I set up the local Share Wokingham site that runs at the FBC every Tuesday; ran a Welcome course for HongKongers new to the community and organised sports sessions for refugees. Standing to represent Finchampstead seems a natural next step in serving the community.

I'm originally from France and have lived in Finchampstead with my wife and two daughters for 19 years. I've worked in high-tech companies, and volunteered at Gorse Ride Schools as a parent governor. I want to make Finchampstead a thriving community and it would be an honour to serve as a Borough Councillor. I'll listen to all residents to understand what matters to them, and help make a real difference in Finchampstead. Our community has experienced many changes over the years, with pressures from development and increased traffic. I want to ensure that local residents have a voice.
